kept.text: @RepLuna @alandidio A meeting in an Airbnb in Tennessee with a small group of pastors absolutely happened. Alan DiDio confirmed he was there. But let's not pretend this was some official Pentagon briefing. No named officials, no documents, no recordings, phones on airplane mode. It was just some intelligence-adjacent guys talking to prophecy pastors who sell books about demons and the Book of Enoch. Something weird is definitely going on with UAP, but this version of the story is being sold by people whose entire brand depends on it being the biggest spiritual deception in human history. Take it with a massive grain of salt.

profile_bio.description: I use behavioral psychology & dopamine flywheels to help you perform at your peak & negotiate better deals Ex Pro ⚾ Ex Google Learn more @ https://t.co/V2GvsqhR2k

quoted_tweet.text:
Brian still spends over two hours a day on recruiting and personally hires the top 200 people at Airbnb. 

I loved this idea of being in the flow of talent to find the best people:

"Don't do searches. Build pipelines. I try to map out all the best people in the Valley.

So let's say I need to hire really good engineers. I don't do searches. I just informationally meet the best engineers in the world. Every meeting, the job is to get the next meeting, meet someone else.

The mistake people make when they hire. They go, "I need to hire a blank." So they hire a search firm. They give you 50 profiles, and you pick the best one. That is the wrong way to do it.

The best way to do it is pipeline recruiting. You're constantly recruiting, you're constantly meeting people. in advance of searches. And all of it is referral based.

The two ways to find out if people are good – is to start with the results and work backwards to the people. 

Find an ad you like and figure out who made that ad. Start with the results. Work backwards to people. Don't start with the resume.

The other thing to do is just keep asking people to build your Rolodex. The moment I find somebody that's really good, I ask them who all the best people they know are. 

And I build these little mafias and they tell you who the other good people are.

I am the co-hiring manager for the top 200 people in the company. This is very radical. A lot of CEOs think it's their job to hire their executive team, and their executive team hires their team. I think that is fatal. 

You always want to be marrying up, hiring people of the future. It should be like we're reaching. If you can hire them without my help, we're not reaching far enough. You want to hire the very best person you can."

kept.text: Skoro prowadzą te airbnb w zgodzie z sąsiadami to w sumie co im przeszkadza że będzie wymagana zgoda wspólnoty 🤔 https://t.co/DiHuYhKc6A

Airbnb CEO Brian Cheskyの「AI Founder Mode」の話が面白かった。

そもそもFounder Modeって、過剰な権限委譲をやめて、自分のやり方で会社を運営することを謝らない、ディテールに戻る、というシンプルな話。Cheskyの場合は、パンデミックで8週間で売上の80%が消えた瞬間に切り替わったそうです。そこから2〜3年、週100時間、すべての意思決定を自分でレビューしたと。

AI Founder Modeはこれよりさらに激しい。理由はメカニカルで、Founder Modeのボトルネックは「会議」だった。AIがあれば情報はオンデマンドで取れる。会議文化は非同期に置き換わり、マネジメントレイヤーも一気に薄くなる。

AI時代に生き残れないリーダーは2タイプ：
 ・ピュアなピープルマネージャー。「1on1とセラピー」型のリーダーシップはもう終わり。
 ・やり方を変えられない、硬直した人。

これからは全員がプレイングマネージャー。エンジニアはコードを書き、弁護士は判例を読み、デザイナーはデザインをする。「You manage people through the work. You don't manage the people, you manage the work.」
直感とディテールで会社を回してきた創業者は、ここ10年「やりすぎ」と見られがちだった。

次の10年は、その人たちの時代になる。

قامت هذه السيدة بحجز فندق عبر Airbnb، وكانت تتوقع ليلة هادئة تنهي بها يومًا طويلًا من الإرهاق.

وصلت في وقت متأخر، بدا المكان طبيعيًا في البداية، هدوء تام يحيط بكل شيء، وكأن لا شيء يدعو للقلق.

لكن بعد دقائق بدأت تسمع صوتًا غريبًا.
توقفت فجأة، حاولت تجاهله، وأقنعت نفسها أنه مجرد وهم بسبب التعب… لكن الصوت عاد مرة أخرى، أوضح هذه المرة.

لم يكن ثابتًا… أحيانًا يقترب، وأحيانًا يختفي، وكأنه يتحرك في أرجاء المكان.

بدأ التوتر يتسلل إليها، وتجولت بحذر محاولة معرفة مصدره، لكن دون أي نتيجة.

كل زاوية بدت صامتة 💀بشكل مريب.

شعرت أن البقاء في صمت دون فهم ما يحدث أسوأ من المواجهة،
فقررت أن توثق كل شيء.

أخرجت هاتفها، وبدأت التصوير
وهذا هو المقطع الذي سجلته، حيث يظهر الصوت الغامض بوضوح ويزيد من حدة التوتر.

kept.text:
If I booked a room anywhere may it be a hotel or an Airbnb, I would expect everything to be in working order. Anything less than that would be unsatisfactory for me.

This woman is showing everyone that her Airbnb doesn’t even have running water. How does anyone not know that their place for rent does t have water. I would be livid if I came to sleep, it’s after midnight and when I go to take a shower I find out there’s no water.

Now obviously I wouldn’t pay full price for anything unlivable such as this. However if I stayed I would have expected a discount but in the morning I would have just left. However would it be unreasonable to expect this visit to be free, after all, the renter wouldn’t be able to use the restroom either?

profile_bio.description: Having fun while spending time with my kids. Life is what you make of it.

quoted_tweet.text:
In a situation like this I feel it’s best to just get out, better safe than sorry. I rather be in a safe location and ask the questions that need to be asked and find out the facts later. It’s hard to gauge who is telling the truth in this situation and the ones who would be paying the price would be us, the renter.

She got a message from Airbnb telling her they need to leave immediately, as soon as she got the message she left but also got a message from the host telling her not to believe it and it was a false allegation.

I feel the whole situation could be dangerous and off putting, who could I trust to tell me the truth. The only thing that would matter to me is that I get out and later on I get my money back, but after something like this I think I would feel safer just using a hotel, do you feel the same, would you have stayed?

kept.text:
Brian Chesky offers a contrarian view: obsessing over growth is the wrong thing for early founders to focus on.

Brian is asked how he pushed through the challenge of slow growth while still keeping his team moving at pace.

He starts with a counterintuitive observation:

"When you build a marketplace and you have no users, you have a lot of time on your hands."

@bchesky recalls sitting in the kitchen late at night with his team, wondering why Airbnb wasn't growing fast enough.

He'd compare himself to founder friends with millions of users and spiral through the same questions on repeat.

"I have friends, they have millions of users. Why doesn't anyone like us? Are we not smart enough? Is our product not good enough? Do we not have a good enough idea?"

Two things made a huge difference for him.

The first was having a good adviser — a startup founder from another Y Combinator company who he met with every week.

The weekly cadence reframed how Brian thought about progress:

"Each meeting I just try to show progress and he's like don't focus on how many users you have, focus on continually making progress."

The second was a shift in perspective on what having few users actually meant:

"Looking back, [it was] a blessing not having a lot of users because you can focus on the few people you have and just constantly making them fall more in love with your product."

Brian's takeaway flips the conventional wisdom on its head:

"I used to be obsessed with growth and looking back it was the wrong thing to focus on."

kept.text:
Web3 failed because everyone, from builders to users, tried to take one quick win and leave.

What kind of service in the world exists only for people looking for a quick cash grab?

It is easy to forget, but remember this:

Airbnb, YouTube, X, Facebook, and Instagram have all been around for more than 10 years.

And in their early days, they were so rough that it would have been hard to imagine them becoming the services they are today.

The reason they still exist is because they were obsessed with sustainability.

This is a YouTube video from 20 years ago.

kept.text:
Tři daňové chyby pronajímatelů, které stojí desítky tisíc ročně:

→ Krátkodobý pronájem (Airbnb, Booking) ve většině případů patří mezi podnikání, ne mezi klasický pronájem. Pronajímatel platí navíc sociální a zdravotní odvody. Při milionovém ročním příjmu rozdíl klidně přes 100 000 Kč.

→ Darovaný byt v rodině nelze podle zákona vůbec daňově odpisovat. Pronajímatel přichází ročně o tisíce, které by jinak ušetřil na dani z příjmu.

→ Zděděný byt odpisovat lze. Stejný byt, stejná rodina, jiný způsob převodu = úplně jiný daňový osud.

Daňový poradce Daniel Vladyka rozebírá v novém videu, kde v daňovém přiznání nejčastěji utíkají peníze. 40 minut, co se ti můžou vrátit násobně. 

O webinář byl obrovský zájem a není se co divit – vyhnout se těmhle chybám může znamenat stovky tisíc v kapse.

kept.text:
とりあえずこの宿に泊まってみて？

・民泊運営始めたいけどってどんなものかよく分からない
・自分の民泊の清掃クオリティが低い
・温泉でのんびりしたい😍

そんなあなたにおすすめするのはこの宿！
オープンしてから1年が経つけど、私の集大成のうちの1つだと思ってる。
自動メッセージ、チェックインガイド、ハウスマニュアル、お部屋のしつらえ、全てにおいて参考になると思う。

少し前に苦肉の策でホストキャンセルしたらゴールド落ちしちゃったけど😭(ホストキャンセルはやはりゲストチョイスに影響あり)

2泊以上してのんびりするのがおすすめ。私が伊豆に行くときに決まって泊まるのもここ。居心地がとても良いのよね。

インバウンドゲストの比率がめちゃくちゃ高いのもこの宿。
参考にクリッククリック👇
https://t.co/CVSEO8niCe

quoted_tweet.text:
Bonjour @ameliembk_  , j’espère que tu as passé un bon week-end de Saint Fally.
Je préfère te prévenir pour que cela ne te surprenne pas : une plainte pour diffamation a été déposée contre toi suite à tes déclarations publiques.

Je te conseille de préparer tes avocats.
À très bientôt.
